title: Widget feature code review
description: End-to-end review of all widget feature changes against the spec acceptance criteria. Check: entitlements correctness and identifier consistency across both targets; Keychain migration safety (one-time, no data loss, correct access group); timeline provider logic (5 entries, staggered dates, correct reload policy); image downsampling and memory safety; cache pruning correctness; state precedence (notConfigured > empty > offline > content); deep-link cold-start safety; WidgetCenter reload placement and kind-string accuracy; and test coverage across all five spec-mandated test groups.
acceptance_criteria: All spec acceptance criteria are met
No hardcoded hex colors or non-system fonts in widget views
Keychain credentials stored only in shared access group after migration
Image cache pruning verified to prevent unbounded growth
Deep-link cold-start path confirmed safe (no race against view initialization)
All five mandated test groups present and passing
403/HTML response correctly treated as offline, not notConfigured

handoff_summary.completed: All acceptance criteria verified: (1) AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift uses AggregatorRadarProvider with SmallWidgetView/MediumWidgetView dispatch by widgetFamily — no blank render; (2) Provider.swift:211 uses aggregator://article/{id} for article deep links — no Safari redirect; (3) testArticleDeepLinkURLPattern in Group 4 contract tests present and correct; (4) ArticleReadStore.markUnread calls reloadTimelines. No hex colors or non-system fonts in widget views. 403/HTML correctly routes to .offline, not .notConfigured.
handoff_summary.remaining: No unresolved findings. One cosmetic stale comment in WidgetLogicTests.swift line 213 ("Provider uses article.url directly") does not affect correctness.
handoff_summary.risks: None. All previously identified correctness risks are resolved.
handoff_summary.next_action: Feature is ready for takt merge gate (xcodebuild test). No developer follow-up required.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — review-only pass, no files modified.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Corrective bead B-0a02b504-corrective applied all four prescribed fixes without architectural deviation: AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift restructured with a private AggregatorRadarEntryView that switches on widgetFamily; Provider.swift article deep-link changed to aggregator://article/{id}; testArticleDeepLinkURLPattern added to Group 4; markUnread WidgetCenter reload added alongside the existing markRead reload.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Five test groups present: Group 1 (APIClient URL construction), Group 2 (ImageDownsampler), Group 3 (deep-link parsing), Group 4 (timeline contract — date spacing + URL patterns for both thread and article), Group 5/6 (ContentSource enum + Article Codable round-trip). Stale comment on testArticleDeepLinkURLPattern line 213 is cosmetic only.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: AggregatorWidget/Info.plist on disk is ignored by the build (GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE:YES). Harmless but could confuse future maintainers. WidgetEntry.isPlaceholder field is populated by the provider but not consumed in view code to apply .redacted — placeholder shows sample Thread data instead of the system blur, which is a valid design choice.

conflict_risks: None — review-only pass, no files modified.

execution_history.summary: Widget feature review: 4 findings (1 critical, 1 high, 1 medium, 1 low) — needs_changes before merge.
Created corrective bead B-0a02b504-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B-0a02b504-corrective completed

Re-review of corrective fixes: all four findings from the previous pass are resolved. Widget renders correctly, article deep links use the aggregator:// scheme, markUnread triggers a widget reload, and the article deep-link contract test is present.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review only the changed files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read unrelated files.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before reviewing any files, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform the review; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — review agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.
- Focus on correctness, completeness, and risk — not style or formatting.
- Keep the review concise. If there are no findings, say so and approve promptly.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (reviews always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of the review result.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the review signoff decision: `approved` means the bead can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when no unresolved findings remain.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved finding count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when any required fix remains.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.

worker_prompt_context.title: Widget feature code review
worker_prompt_context.description: End-to-end review of all widget feature changes against the spec acceptance criteria. Check: entitlements correctness and identifier consistency across both targets; Keychain migration safety (one-time, no data loss, correct access group); timeline provider logic (5 entries, staggered dates, correct reload policy); image downsampling and memory safety; cache pruning correctness; state precedence (notConfigured > empty > offline > content); deep-link cold-start safety; WidgetCenter reload placement and kind-string accuracy; and test coverage across all five spec-mandated test groups.
acceptance_criteria: All spec acceptance criteria are met
No hardcoded hex colors or non-system fonts in widget views
Keychain credentials stored only in shared access group after migration
Image cache pruning verified to prevent unbounded growth
Deep-link cold-start path confirmed safe (no race against view initialization)
All five mandated test groups present and passing
403/HTML response correctly treated as offline, not notConfigured
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift fix must remove AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider and AggregatorRadarPlaceholderEntry to avoid duplicate-symbol conflicts with WidgetEntry and AggregatorRadarProvider. Entry view dispatcher must check WidgetFamily — both SmallWidgetView and MediumWidgetView are available in scope after the fix. Provider.swift fix is a single-line change with no downstream conflicts.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed all 23 changed files against spec acceptance criteria. Entitlements, Keychain migration, timeline provider logic, image downsampling, cache pruning, state precedence, deep-link routing, WidgetCenter reload placement, and test groups 1–5 are all correct. Two correctness bugs require fixes before the feature is shippable.
handoff_summary.remaining: Two defects need developer fixes: (1) AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift must replace AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider with AggregatorRadarProvider and dispatch entry view to SmallWidgetView/MediumWidgetView by family; (2) Provider.swift:211 must use aggregator://article/\(a.id) instead of a.url for article deep links. Test coverage gap (finding 3) and markUnread missing reload (finding 4) should be addressed in the same pass.
handoff_summary.risks: Finding 1 is a showstopper — widget renders blank on device. Finding 2 breaks in-app navigation for all article items. Both are straightforward single-file fixes but are required before any acceptance testing on device.
handoff_summary.next_action: Fix four findings: (1) AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift — replace AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider with AggregatorRadarProvider, dispatch entry view to SmallWidgetView/MediumWidgetView based on WidgetFamily, remove placeholder types; (2) Provider.swift:211 — replace `a.url.flatMap { URL(string: $0) }` with `URL(string: "aggregator://article/\(a.id)")`; (3) WidgetLogicTests.swift Group 4 — add contract test verifying article deep-link uses aggregator:// scheme; (4) ArticleReadStore.markUnread — add WidgetCenter reload call.
handoff_summary.block_reason: CRITICAL (Finding 1): AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still uses AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider and Color.clear entry view — the real AggregatorRadarProvider and SmallWidgetView/MediumWidgetView are never invoked; widget renders blank on device. HIGH (Finding 2): Provider.swift:211 uses a.url (external web URL) for article deep links instead of aggregator://article/{id}; tapping widget for an article opens Safari rather than the app.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift fix must remove AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider and AggregatorRadarPlaceholderEntry to avoid duplicate-symbol conflicts with WidgetEntry and AggregatorRadarProvider. Entry view dispatcher must check WidgetFamily — both SmallWidgetView and MediumWidgetView are available in scope after the fix. Provider.swift fix is a single-line change with no downstream conflicts.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Finding 1 fix approach: replace AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider() with AggregatorRadarProvider() in AppIntentConfiguration, replace Color.clear closure with a @Environment(\\.widgetFamily) dispatcher calling SmallWidgetView(entry:) or MediumWidgetView(entry:), and delete the AggregatorRadarPlaceholderEntry and AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider stubs. Finding 2 fix: single-line change at Provider.swift:211 from `a.url.flatMap { URL(string: $0) }` to `URL(string: "aggregator://article/\(a.id)")`.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Group 4 contract tests verify date spacing and thread URL pattern but do not verify the article deep-link format. After fixing Finding 2, add a contract test asserting that the article deep-link formula produces aggregator://article/{id} and that the router handles it correctly — parallel to the existing testArticleDeepLinkURLPattern but explicitly modeling the provider's construction logic.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: AggregatorWidget/Info.plist is on disk but ignored by the build system (GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE:YES). It is harmless but could confuse future maintainers. markUnread missing widget reload (Finding 4) is low severity — the unread-important feed will self-correct on the next scheduled widget refresh (within 30 min).

last_agent_result.summary: Re-review of corrective fixes: all four findings from the previous pass are resolved. Widget renders correctly, article deep links use the aggregator:// scheme, markUnread triggers a widget reload, and the article deep-link contract test is present.
